page.content: The analysis presents data segmented by age group regarding the correlation of EV with age and IOD. For Children (n = 515), the correlation for OS is 0.114 with a p-value of 0.010, and for IOD, it is 0.607 with a p-value of <0.001; for OD, the correlation remains 0.114 with a p-value of 0.010, while for IOD, it is 0.606 with a p-value of <0.001. Teenagers (n = 517) show a correlation of 0.207 for OS with a p-value of <0.001 and 0.365 for IOD with a p-value of <0.001; for OD, the correlation is 0.217 with a p-value of <0.001 and 0.372 for IOD with a p-value of <0.001. Adults (n = 582) exhibit a correlation of 0.038 for OS with a p-value of 0.366 and 0.318 for IOD with a p-value of <0.001; for OD, the correlation is 0.027 with a p-value of 0.511 and 0.323 for IOD with a p-value of <0.001.
